sources_audited.note: The exact Census URL resolves to a general 2022 NAICS search page and does not display the 336213 definition or the stated inclusions and exclusions. The lens remains coherent, but this exact citation does not substantiate it.
The exact data.census.gov profile loads only a JavaScript-required shell in the reopened source, so the claimed 71 employer establishments cannot be confirmed from the cited page.
The May 2023 OEWS page is explicitly for NAICS 336200 and reports production occupations at 65.21% and assemblers and fabricators at 31.75%, matching the stated broad proxy and caveat.
The FRED annual industrial-production series is specific to NAICS 336213 and reports 122.5407 in 2022, 87.3771 in 2023, 65.8724 in 2024, and 73.3577 in 2025.

The FRED page supports that this is the monthly BLS PPI for motor-home manufacturing and reports 230.211 for May 2026, but a selling-price index does not support the estimated 0.59 AI value-retention rate. The packet labels q ESTIMATE and discloses this limitation.

The RVIA page supports 36,029 motor-home shipments in 2025 and 3.3% growth in its prose, but it does not support the separate rationale claim that early-2026 motor-home shipments were up 17.7% through February.

BEA reports RVing as the second-largest conventional outdoor activity with $27.5 billion of current-dollar value added in 2024; the packet appropriately discloses that this is far broader than motor-home purchases.

The Census landing page confirms 2024 ABS employer-business owner-characteristics tables and an Age of Owner table, but it does not measure motor-home-manufacturer control transfers or support the numeric s5 estimate. The primitive is honestly labeled PROXY with a directional bridge.

The NHTSA interpretation supports final-stage-manufacturer duties to complete vehicles to applicable federal safety standards and affix a certification label, but it is a 1995 interpretation involving used chassis-cabs rather than a motor-home-specific implementation study and does not directly support the numeric rho or o estimates.

caveats: The exact S0 Census URL does not display the claimed 336213 definition, so the otherwise coherent default lens lacks direct support from that citation.
The exact S1 data.census.gov URL exposes only a JavaScript shell to the audit, leaving the claimed 71-establishment count unverified from the cited page.
S4 documents an industry PPI but not AI value retention; q remains an honest bounded estimate whose limitation is disclosed.

The early-2026 17.7% motor-home shipment-growth statistic in the d5 rationale is not supported by S5, although the cited 2025 total and growth are supported; removing the extra statistic would not change the base tier or action.
S7 supplies only a broad owner-age table rather than a sector-specific transfer rate; the packet's PROXY label, bridge, range, and caveats are appropriate.
S8 supports final-stage certification obligations but is not motor-home-specific and does not establish the exact rho or o values; both affected primitives are labeled ESTIMATE.
summary: Mechanics pass. The record is useful and candidly model-conditioned: direct source claims for the broad occupation mix, industrial-production history, 2025 motor-home shipments, broad RVing value added, and final-stage certification are supported, while the major score-driving transformations are labeled PROXY or ESTIMATE with wide ranges and explicit caveats. Exact support is incomplete for the NAICS-definition URL, the 71-establishment profile, AI retention, sector-specific succession, and one early-2026 shipment statistic. None of those defects, corrected or downgraded honestly, would change the base STRUCTURAL_OUT tier or the VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action, so they are caveats rather than material findings.
